tease: Slow track, set a target — the oldest cricket rule. The IPL 2026 data has a different idea.
hook: Of 4 fixtures with a quiet 1st-innings powerplay (boundaries < 10%), the team batting second won **3**.
detail: Conventional read: 'slow track, set a target.' The data: a slow powerplay points to the chasing team, not the defending one. 75% chase win-rate when the openers can't find the rope.

tease: "They finished strong, that's a defendable total." Or is it.
hook: When the team batting first finishes with a **>180 SR death** phase, the chasing team still wins 61% of the time.
detail: 14 of 23 captured fixtures. The narrative says "they posted a defendable score." The data says modern T20 chases over par regardless of how the first innings finished.

numbers.label: Fixtures with >180 SR death phase (1st inn)

tease: First-innings total isn't the strongest defeat signal this IPL. Something quieter is.
hook: Teams whose 1st-innings death SR fell **>20 points below** their powerplay SR have won only 59% of those matches this season.
detail: 10 of 17 captured fixtures. Loss of momentum in overs 16-20 — even from a hot powerplay start — is a stronger signal of defeat than first-innings total.

numbers.label: Fixtures where 1st-inn decelerated (death SR > 20 below PP)

tease: Not every pitch rewards a chase. The IPL 2026 data has venues that flatly defend.
hook: M.Chinnaswamy Stadium: 1-of-5 chases successful, **18%** average powerplay-boundary rate.
detail: 5 captured fixtures. The PP boundary rate is the pitch's signature — 18% means two-paced. Chase rate 20% confirms the read.

tease: A two-paced venue. The 1st-innings powerplay tells you which way the night will lean.
hook: Wankhede Stadium: 4-of-6 chases successful, **31%** average powerplay-boundary rate.
detail: 6 captured fixtures. The PP boundary rate is the pitch's signature — 31% means true batting deck. Chase rate 67% confirms the read.

tease: Par changes by ground. The headline 'good' total at one venue is below par at another.
hook: M.Chinnaswamy Stadium: 1st-innings par **195** across 5 captured fixtures (high 250, low 146).
detail: Par defines the chase math. 195 is the average 1st-innings total at M.Chinnaswamy Stadium. Sub-par defends only with bowling collapse; over-par flips chase win-rate.

tease: Toss decisions at one ground are rituals. The captain who picked second usually wins here.
hook: M.Chinnaswamy Stadium captains: **100%** elect to bowl; bowl-electors win **80%** of those.
detail: 0 of 5 elected to bat first (winning 0); 5 elected to bowl (winning 4). The toss-elect → win pattern at M.Chinnaswamy Stadium is a real signal.

tease: Toss decisions at one ground are rituals. The captain who picked second usually wins here.

detail: 1 of 6 elected to bat first (winning 0); 5 elected to bowl (winning 3). The toss-elect → win pattern at Wankhede Stadium is a real signal.

tease: The toss decision matters more than commentary admits this IPL.
hook: Captains who won the toss and ELECTED TO BAT: 11% win rate. Captains who chose to BOWL: **60%**.
detail: 9 'we'll bat' decisions + 48 'we'll bowl' decisions across the captured season. The dew-and-grip math is well-known. The magnitude this season — 49-point edge — isn't.

tease: The toss is supposed to be a coin flip. This IPL is testing that.
hook: Toss winners are converting **53%** of matches into wins this IPL 2026.
detail: Across 57 captured fixtures: toss won → match won in 30 of them. The toss is supposed to be 50/50; this season it's a 53/47 signal.

tease: The 200-target stopped meaning what it used to. There's a tell.
hook: 11 fixtures this season chased **200+** successfully. All had powerplay SR ≈ **190**.
detail: Targets cleared: 210, 204, 209, 222, 265, 229, 228, 249, 226, 216, 205. Average chase powerplay SR across these: 190. The ceiling for a "set total" has moved by 30-40 runs this IPL — and the powerplay tells you if it'll be cleared.

tease: Live commentary calls these "shocks." The data calls them a pattern.
hook: 7 sub-160 totals successfully defended this season — and the chasing team lost their powerplay every time.
detail: Defended totals: 127, 150, 146, 155, 158, 155, 159. Average chase powerplay wickets in those games: 2.6. Live commentary calls these "shocks." Powerplay wickets ÷ defendable score = the actual signal.
